name: local-model-showcase
description: Local-model: the zero-dependency flywheel harness that gates every release with honest, re-readable receipts, all on a home machine.

steps.title: What the harness is
steps.command: echo local-model - the zero-dependency flywheel harness and its release receipts
steps.narration: This is the workshop the models are built in. A single front controller, running on a home machine, that turns readiness checks and benchmarks into receipts you can read on disk.

steps.title: The front controller
steps.narration: One dispatcher, and every lane of the flywheel opens from it: the executable manifest, the command registry, endpoint gates, package doctors, and the model publish path. No dependencies, no cloud, just the whole surface in one place.

steps.title: The release readiness receipt
steps.narration: Before anything ships, the harness writes itself this readiness receipt. This is the real artifact from disk, not a slide. It states plainly what is ready and what still stands between the model and release.

steps.title: The endpoint gate receipt
steps.narration: And the endpoint gate is the health check for the local model servers, recorded the same honest way. A small file that remembers exactly what was observed the last time the gate ran.
